secondaries.headline: Quoting Seth Larson
secondaries.blurb: The Python Package Index (PyPI) now rejects new files being uploaded to releases that are older than 14 days. This restriction was put in place to prevent old and long-stable releases from being poisoned in case publishing tokens or workflows of PyPI projects were compromised. As far as we are aware this has not yet been abused, but there is no technical reason beyond that attackers weren't aware

stories.headline: Quoting Thomas Ptacek
stories.blurb: I genuinely believe that if you took an open weights model from 2025 and built a pentest harness for it, it could do this kind of sandbox escape and scan/hack in most networks. This is only surprising because you assume OpenAI has sounder sandboxes. — Thomas Ptacek , doesn't think this even needs a frontier model Tags: thomas-ptacek , openai , security , generative-ai , ai-security-research , ai ,

stories.headline: OpenAI’s accidental cyberattack against Hugging Face is science fiction that happened
stories.blurb: This story is wild. The short version: OpenAI were running a cybersecurity test against an unreleased model, with the model's guardrail features turned off. Rather than solve the test, the model broke its way out of OpenAI's sandbox, then found exploits to break in to Hugging Face, all so it could cheat on the test by stealing the answers. Along the way it helped make the strongest case yet for ho

stories.headline: Are AI labs pelicanmaxxing?
stories.blurb: Are AI labs pelicanmaxxing? Excellent piece of work by Dylan Castillo, who took a deep-dive into the frequently pondered question of whether the AI labs have been deliberately training models to draw pelicans riding bicycles in response to my deeply unscientific benchmark . I've been randomly spot-checking this in the past by testing models against other animals riding other types of vehicle, but

stories.headline: Orchestrions
stories.blurb: San Francisco tip: it only costs around $15 ($10 in quarters plus a $5 bill for the self-playing violin) to activate every single Orchestrion in Musée Mécanique . And because most people are bad at allocating their funds you may well be the ONLY person activating the Orchestrions, which means you get to craft the soundscape for the entire museum. Tags: san-francisco

stories.headline: Where Did This Tik To Sound Come From? IYO Meme Explained
stories.blurb: Iyo, often capitalized as iyo, is a viral TikTok sound and nonsensical slang term originating from a Fortnite voice chat clip featuring various young-sounding players engaging in an increasingly heated exchange of trash talk. The audio includes the line, "If you don't stop talking, I'mma get a little KK to drop a little JJ," followed by one of the players saying, "Iyo," a distinctive pronunciation

stories.blurb: Foam Party Girl or the Goddamnitdonut Look and Bounce Video, also known as the Ann K. Video, is a viral video of a girl at a foam party, purported to be Instagram user goddamnitdonut, aka Ann K., dancing and then giving an unseen person a flirtatious look before she continues dancing. stories.headline: Internet Mocks Andrew Tate Arrest Outfit
stories.blurb: Andrew Tate Arrest Outfit, also known as the Andrew Tate Arrest Outfit Meme, refers to a series of memes and jokes mocking the clothing worn by manosphere podcaster and influencer Andrew Tate in photographs taken during his July 2026 arrest in Miami. stories.headline: The 'Marc Cucurella Cat' Meme Becomes One Of The Last Major World Cup Memes
stories.blurb: Marc Cucurella Cat, also known simply as the Cucurella Cat Meme, is a viral video and reaction image of a tabby cat sitting on a windowsill wearing a large black curly wig, resembling Spanish professional footballer and soccer star Marc Cucurella Saseta, who plays for the La Liga club Real Madrid and Spain's men's national team. stories.headline: Kevin Warsh has homed in on three key phrases. How Fed watchers interpret them
stories.blurb: In five public appearances, Warsh has used the phrase "family fight" 13 times, returning to "first principles" 11 times and "inflation is a choice" for the Fed six times.

stories.headline: Google Fined $1 Billion by EU for Search Bias and App Store Restrictions
stories.blurb: Google was today fined €890 million ($1 billion) by the European Union for breaching antitrust rules with regard to its search and Google Play store services. The European Commission found that Google had broken the Digital Markets Act (DMA) by using its dominant position in the search market to favour its own services, like shopping and hotel deals, over those of rivals. The Commission also said

stories.headline: Foldable iPhone Still Faces Production Hurdles, Report Says
stories.blurb: Apple's foldable iPhone still faces production hurdles that leave its exact launch timing undecided, according to a new report from DigiTimes , even as Foxconn carries out what supply chain sources describe as "final mass-production adjustments."
